name: Villatoro and Garganta de los Hornos Loop
name_original: Garganta de Hornos y Navacepedilla del Corneja — circular desde Villatoro
description: This difficult 22.9-mile mountain biking route near Villatoro offers challenging terrain and scenic views with 3832 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Villatoro, located in the Ávila province of Spain, offers diverse terrain for outdoor enthusiasts. The region is characterized by the Sierra de la Almijara mountains, providing significant elevation changes and varied landscapes. Riders can expect a mix of lush forests, natural streams, and panoramic viewpoints across the mountainous environment. This area provides a rich playground for mountain bike trails Villatoro, with routes traversing natural features. Best mountain bike trails around Villatoro The most popular mountain bike trail is Villatoro and Garganta de los Hornos Loop, a 22.9 miles (36.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 4 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the natural landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Peña del Cuervo – Puerto de Villatoro loop from Puerto de Villatoro, a difficult 32.9 miles (52.9 km) path. This route offers extensive riding through varied terrain, including open areas and natural features. Local mountain bikers also love the Arroyo Prodesagar – Rollo de Muñotello loop from Villatoro, a 43.6 miles (70.2 km) trail leading through the Arroyo Prodesagar area, often completed in about 6 hours 29 minutes. Mountain biking around Villatoro is defined by mountainous terrain, lush forests, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, including moderate and difficult mountain bike trails. The routes in Villatoro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 50 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villatoro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Villatoro?
A: There are nearly 20 mountain bike trails around Villatoro, offering diverse terrain for riders. These routes traverse the picturesque landscapes of the Sierra de la Almijara mountains, providing a mix of lush forests, natural streams, and panoramic viewpoints.

Q: What is the terrain like for mountain biking around Villatoro?
A: The terrain around Villatoro is characterized by the Sierra de la Almijara mountains, offering significant elevation changes and varied landscapes. You can expect a mix of lush forests, natural streams, and panoramic viewpoints, making for an exhilarating mountain biking experience.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes in Villatoro?
A: Yes, Villatoro offers several challenging mountain bike routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1087726997/garganta-de-hornos-y-navacepedilla-del-corneja-circular-desde-villatoro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garganta de Hornos y Navacepedilla del Corneja — circular desde Villatoro&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 22.9-mile (36.8 km) trail with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38153772" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino del Tío Alberto – Bridge of the Seven Eyes loop from Navacepedilla de Corneja&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 35.1 miles (56.5 km) through varied terrain.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails available?
A: Yes, out of the 19 available routes, 11 are classified as moderate, providing options for riders looking for a less strenuous but still engaging experience. These trails still allow you to enjoy the natural beauty of the region without the extreme demands of the difficult routes.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The trails often lead through lush forests and past natural streams. You can also encounter notable landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1711130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chía Pass (1,663 m)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5585730" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Fuentes Pass&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5539755" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serrota&lt;/a&gt; summit is another prominent natural feature in the area.

Q: Are there any historical sites or man-made monuments along the routes?
A: Yes, some routes pass by interesting historical sites. For example, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4699488" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rollo de Muñotello&lt;/a&gt;, a man-made monument, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2876933" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Monasterio del Risco&lt;/a&gt;, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that form a loop?
A: Yes, many routes around Villatoro are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10412599" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo Prodesagar – Rollo de Muñotello loop from Villatoro&lt;/a&gt; is a 43.6-mile (70.2 km) circular trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31504794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Cuervo – Puerto de Villatoro loop from Puerto de Villatoro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Villatoro?
A: The mountain bike trails in Villatoro are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from 8 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the beautiful mountainous scenery, and the well-maintained paths that allow for an immersive outdoor experience.

Q: Is there a route that explores the source of the Adaja River?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31503995" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villatoro – Source of the Adaja River loop from Villatoro&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 29.6-mile (29.6 km) trail that takes you to the source of the Adaja River, offering a unique natural highlight.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Villatoro?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the region's description of lush forests and sparkling streams suggests that spring and autumn would be ideal for mountain biking, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer might also be suitable, especially at higher elevations, but always check local weather conditions.

Q: Are there options for parking or public transport access to the trails?
A: Villatoro is a charming village, and many routes start directly from or near the village, making access convenient. While specific parking details for each trailhead are not provided here, the village itself typically offers parking options. For public transport, local services might connect to nearby towns, but a car is generally recommended for flexibility in accessing various trailheads in the mountainous region.
tips: The Puente or Tío Eduardo mill is part of the ethnographic ensemble of the Corneja river mills. It is a construction of the fourteenth century, the bridge has a semicircular arch and the building collected the water from the river by means of a cacera that brought pressure to the bucket and produced light for the town of Navacepedilla de Corneja by means of a dynamo connected to the mill wheel with a pulley At that time the value of the mill was estimated at 400 reales de vellón.
I recommend a visit to the interior of the mill if it is open when passing by.

faqs.question: What level of fitness and skill is required for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is considered difficult and requires very good fitness and advanced riding skills. It's a challenging mountain bike ride best suited for experienced riders.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The estimated time to complete this circular mountain bike route is around 4 hours and 4 minutes, though this can vary based on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail in Villatoro?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Villatoro serves as the starting point for this circular route. You should be able to find suitable parking within or on the outskirts of the village.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Garganta de Hornos y Navacepedilla del Corneja route?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and significant elevation gain, you can expect varied and potentially technical terrain. The area is known for its mountainous landscapes, so prepare for a mix of surfaces typical of Spanish mountain biking.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several interesting spots. You'll encounter the Garganta de los Hornos Village, the historic Molino del Puente or Tío Eduardo's Mill, and the Tío Alberto Mill.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in this region, avoiding the extreme heat of summer and potential snow or harsh cold in winter. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are often allowed on trails in Spain, especially in natural areas, specific leash rules or restrictions for this particular route are not detailed. It's always best to keep your dog under control and respect local wildlife and other trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is located within the Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos. Generally, there are no entrance fees for accessing the natural park itself for activities like mountain biking, nor are specific permits usually required for individual trails. However, always check for any updated local regulations or specific restrictions, especially concerning protected areas.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, a small section (approximately 6%) of this route overlaps with the official trail known as [CIMA AV01] Chía.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation nearby in Villatoro?
faqs.answer: Villatoro is a small village, and while specific amenities aren't listed, it's a good idea to check local listings for cafes, pubs, or accommodation options if you plan to stay overnight or refuel before/after your ride. Navacepedilla del Corneja might also offer some basic services.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route traverses mountainous landscapes in the Ávila region, so you can anticipate scenic views typical of the Spanish mountains, offering expansive vistas and natural beauty throughout your ride.
